key_risks: Soft CPI/PPI prints spark a risk-on rebound that lifts beaten-down tech/growth and lags defensives.
Healthcare-specific headline or policy surprise reverses its recent leadership.
Continued broad equity selloff drags even defensive sectors lower versus cash.

portfolio.rationale: Defensive sector with strong recent relative strength (+5.6% 7d) amid broad tech selloff; low macro sensitivity into a heavy data week.
Low-vol factor outperformed (+2.8% 7d) and offers defensive equity exposure ahead of CPI/PPI and FOMC eve volatility.
Defensive staples (+2.8% 7d) provide ballast against the tech-led drawdown and sticky inflation prints.
Quality dividend tilt held up flat while growth fell sharply; diversifies defensive equity exposure.
Cash-like buffer to dampen drawdown risk into CPI, PPI, Treasury auctions, and FOMC meeting start.

portfolio_rationale: Tech/growth, semis, crypto, and commodities saw sharp 7d drawdowns with sticky inflation (CPI 3.8% YoY, PPI +6% YoY) and a hawkish hold; defensive factors and sectors are showing clear relative strength into a catalyst-heavy week.
rationale_summary: Tilt defensive into CPI/PPI/FOMC-eve risk via healthcare, low-vol, staples, and dividends with a short-Treasury buffer.
